free_text: Experimental investigation of the 11B + p reaction and
analysis in the frame of the continuum shell model
Targets was prepared from natural boron on a
180 mu-g/cm2 thick gold backing. The averaged value for
target thickness was mu=(22.8+-5) mu-g/cm2, which
corresponds to an energy loss of 2keV at a proton
bombarding energy of Ep=5.5MeV.
The target thickness was determined from the
experimentally measured yield of the (p,p0) reaction
using the well known cross sections for this reaction
from two independent references. In this way the
values mu10=(23.7+-4)mu-g/cm2 for 10B (Kimura, see
REL-REF) and mu11=(21.8+-4.5) mu-g/cm2 for 11B (Watson,
see REL-REF) were obtained.
NE213 liguid scintillators
encapsulated in a 5 in x 1.5 in glass container and a
Si(Li) semiconductor counter with an energy spread of
40 keV was arranged at 150. degr. to the incident
beam in the target chamber
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases
SD: Corrections in all Subents. BIB updated
